sources.name: pile-of-law/pile-of-law
sources.coverage: ~256 GB, 35 sub-corpora: CourtListener opinions (through 12/31/2022), Atticus contracts, BVA, EOIR, USC, CFR, Federal Register, r/legaladvice, EDGAR, casebooks, Canadian decisions, and more
sources.license: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 — NON-COMMERCIAL; disqualifies commercial RAG without renegotiation

sources.name: joelniklaus/Multi_Legal_Pile (+ _Commercial variant)
sources.coverage: 689 GB across 24 EU languages; Native MLP 112GB, Eurlex 179GB, Legal MC4 106GB, Pile-of-Law 292GB
sources.license: Mixed — use Multi_Legal_Pile_Commercial for commercially-safe subset

sources.name: nlpaueb/multi_eurlex (coastalcph/multi_eurlex)
sources.coverage: 65k EU laws in 23 languages; EUROVOC-annotated; chronological split 1958–2016
sources.format: JSON records per CELEX ID with per-language text dict
sources.license: CC BY-SA 4.0 (verify on card) — EU editorial content

sources.name: coastalcph/lex_glue (LexGLUE benchmark)
sources.coverage: 7 tasks: ecthr_a, ecthr_b, scotus, eurlex (EURLEX57K), ledgar, unfair_tos, case_hold

sources.name: dennlinger/eur-lex-sum
sources.coverage: Up to 1,500 doc/summary pairs per language; 375 acts aligned across all 24 EU languages

sources.name: AUEB-NLP/ecthr_cases (+ lex_glue ecthr_a/b)
sources.coverage: ~11k ECtHR cases (train 9k 2001–2016, dev/test 2016–2019); facts-paragraphs + violated/alleged articles + (silver/gold) rationales

sources.name: casehold/casehold
sources.coverage: 53k legal holdings MCQ from Harvard CAP
sources.license: Apache-2.0 code; CC0 underlying data (verify card)

sources.name: FiscalNote/billsum
sources.coverage: 23.5k US Congress + California bills with summaries

sources.name: free-law/Caselaw_Access_Project (+ TeraflopAI, harvard-lil/cold-cases)
sources.coverage: ~6.7M US decisions 1658–2020 (CAP); cold-cases reformats CourtListener bulk with privacy filters
sources.license: CC0 (CAP); cold-cases follows CourtListener removal norms

sources.name: Exploration-Lab/IL-TUR (+ ILDC on GitHub)
sources.coverage: Indian legal benchmark: L-NER, CJPE, legal-statute ID, semantic segmentation; ILDC has 35k SCI cases (GitHub Exploration-Lab/CJPE)

sources.name: irlspbru/RusLawOD
sources.coverage: Russian federal legislation (laws, decrees, resolutions) through late 2025/early 2026; Akoma-Ntoso-inspired XML

sources.name: lexlms/lex_files (LeXFiles)
sources.coverage: 11 English sub-corpora spanning EU, CoE, Canada, US, UK, India; ~6M docs, ~19B tokens — best single multi-jurisdiction English legal corpus

sources.name: alea-institute/kl3m-data-*
sources.coverage: Copyright-clean 132M documents, trillions of tokens, 16 sources (EDGAR 10-Ks, GovInfo, .gov sites)
sources.license: CC BY 4.0 (verify per subset) — strongly commercial-safe

sources.name: theatticusproject/cuad-qa
sources.coverage: 510 commercial contracts, 13,000+ expert annotations across 41 clause categories

sources.name: Helsinki-NLP/un_pc (UN Parallel Corpus)
sources.coverage: UN documents 1990–2014 in 6 UN languages (AR/ZH/EN/FR/RU/ES), manually translated, MT-aligned

sources.name: Knesset Corpus (Goldin et al., 2024)
sources.coverage: 32M sentences / 384M tokens of Israeli parliamentary proceedings; used to fine-tune Knesset-DictaBERT
